findings.evidence: `rg -n "timeSlice_(conj|diagonal|map_zero|im|re)|timeSlice_isPositiveDefiniteKernel|isPositiveDefiniteKernel_conj_symm|isPositiveDefiniteKernel_apply_self_nonneg" code/TauCeti/Analysis/PositiveDefinite -g '*.lean'` finds only `timeSlice_isPositiveDefiniteKernel`, `timeSlice_sum_nonneg`, and `timeSlice_normSq_le` in this file, while `SemigroupGroup.lean:256` and `SemigroupGroup.lean:262` expose the corresponding named origin-level `conj_symm` and `diagonal_nonneg` lemmas.
findings.fix: Add named `timeSlice_conj_symm` (probably `@[simp]`) and `timeSlice_diagonal_nonneg`/`timeSlice_diagonal_im`/`timeSlice_diagonal_re_nonneg`/`timeSlice_diagonal_eq_ofReal_re` analogues, following the existing `IsSemigroupGroupPD.conj_symm` and diagonal API.
findings.issue: The public slice API exposes the kernel predicate and Cauchy--Schwarz bound but no named fixed-time conjugate-symmetry or diagonal real/nonnegative lemmas, so users must re-instantiate generic kernel lemmas and simplify the slice shape for basic identities like `conj (F (t, v - w)) = F (t, w - v)` and `0 ≤ F (t, 0)`.

summary: The finite quadratic-form consequence from the prior review is now exposed, but the fixed-time kernel API still omits the named symmetry and diagonal facts that characterize the slice and are needed for downstream normalization/Bochner use.
